patch.value: SDK ergonomics: package-safe error base, version negotiation, document PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT

SDK-audit findings to make third-party pm package authoring easy and best-practice. (1) Packages hand-redefine a standalone PmCliError whose name must literally equal 'PmCliError' to match Sentry isExpectedCliErrorEvent (pm-calendar/index.ts ~11) — fragile tribal knowledge. Export a package-safe error base / definePmError helper from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk. (2) No capability/version negotiation: manifest_version is parsed but there's no pm_min_version/engines gate, so version skew throws deep in a handler instead of failing clean. (3) Bundled packages re-import core internals via an undocumented PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT + dist/sdk/runtime.js, a pattern third parties cannot replicate — either document it as first-party-only or convert one package to import purely from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk as the canonical example. (4) Reconcile registerOutputService vs registerService('output_format') naming drift between EXTENSIONS.md/SDK.md and actual API. (5) Consider an in-SDK test harness for asserting a registered command's contract.
SDK exports a package-safe error primitive; manifest pm_min_version gate; canonical replicable example package; docs reconciled

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: SDK ergonomics audit 2026-05-31 (deferred from this perf-focused PR; concrete plan for a follow-up): (1) Version negotiation gap — ExtensionManifest (core/extensions/extension-types.ts) has version + optional manifest_version but NO pm_min_version/engines gate; loader.ts loads + activates without any CLI-version compatibility check, so a package using newer SDK features silently loads in an old CLI and fails deep in a handler. Add pm_min_version?:string to the manifest and check it in the loader before activation with a clear diagnostic. (2) PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT is undocumented — set in cli/main.ts, guide.ts, mcp/server.ts and consumed by bundled package runtime-loaders (packages/pm-todos/.../runtime-loader.ts), but absent from docs/SDK.md and docs/EXTENSIONS.md; document it as the first-party mechanism for bundled packages to locate dist assets, and note third-party npm packages must not rely on it. (3) Package-safe error base — packages hand-redefine `class PmCliError extends Error { name='PmCliError' }` to match the Sentry isExpectedCliErrorEvent filter; this is actually the CORRECT pattern (packages cannot value-import from src/sdk once installed), but it is tribal knowledge — document it in the extension authoring guide. None of these block agents today; tracking for a dedicated SDK PR.
